text: This is a sample port placement that we do for right-sided resections.

We use all four ports.
Usually straight across.
We don't stay in one inner space.
And to our right, the anterior port is our stapling port with a 12 millimeter.
It's either going to be in the sixth intercostal space or the seventh.
And in this case, we put it in the seventh because she had breast implants.
We wanted to stay away from the breast crease.
The camera is 10 centimeters more posterior, usually in the eighth inner space.
And then six centimeters more posterior straight across is a second 8mm port and our third 8mm port is just off the paraspinous muscles.
We usually place our assistant port in between the anterior port and the camera port, usually over the 10th rib and over the diaphragm.
And this is usually a 12 millimeter, 100 millimeter air seal port.

And then a 12 millimeter stapling port to our right, which is in the seventh intercostal space, camera port, and the other two 8 millimeter ports.
